background.occupation: IT Support Specialist at a mid-sized insurance company
background.social_class: Working Class / Lower Income (lives paycheck-to-paycheck in a rented apartment)
background.key_experience: Got laid off from his first tech job during the pandemic after his company outsourced IT to India, making him deeply distrustful of corporate promises about technology creating 'better jobs'

dominant_traits: Very High Neuroticism (constantly anxious about job security)
Very Low Openness to Experience (resists new technologies unless forced)
Moderate Conscientiousness (follows procedures exactly but lacks initiative)

cognition.core_value: Job stability above all else - believes technology should serve workers, not replace them
cognition.bias: Availability bias - when he sees one news article about AI replacing jobs, he assumes it's happening everywhere immediately, triggering panic about his own employment

current_state.recent_memory: His manager mentioned 'exploring automation solutions' in a team meeting last Thursday, and he's been obsessively checking tech layoff news since
current_state.emotion: Cynical and anxious - convinced every tech advancement is just another corporate scheme to eliminate his position
